children.heading: Allocation in certain corporate separations and reorganizations
children.label: h Allocation in certain corporate separations and reorganizations

children.heading: Section 355
children.label: 1 Section 355
children.text: In the case of a distribution or exchange to which section 355 (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 355) applies, proper allocation with respect to the earnings and profits of the distributing corporation and the controlled corporation (or corporations) shall be made under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Section 368(a)(1)(C) or (D)
children.label: 2 Section 368(a)(1)(C) or (D)
children.text: In the case of a reorganization described in subparagraph (C) or (D) of section 368(a)(1), proper allocation with respect to the earnings and profits of the acquired corporation shall, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, be made between the acquiring corporation and the acquired corporation (or any corporation which had control of the acquired corporation before the reorganization).

children.heading: Effect of depreciation on earnings and profits
children.label: k Effect of depreciation on earnings and profits

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: For purposes of computing the earnings and profits of a corporation for any taxable year beginning after , the allowance for depreciation (and amortization, if any) shall be deemed to be the amount which would be allowable for such year if the straight line method of depreciation had been used for each taxable year beginning after .June 30, 1972June 30, 1972

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: If for any taxable year a method of depreciation was used by the taxpayer which the Secretary has determined results in a reasonable allowance under section 167(a) and which is the unit-of-production method or other method not expressed in a term of years, then the adjustment to earnings and profits for depreciation for such year shall be determined under the method so used (in lieu of the straight line method).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), in the case of tangible property to which section 168 applies, the adjustment to earnings and profits for depreciation for any taxable year shall be determined under the alternative depreciation system (within the meaning of section 168(g)(2)).

children.heading: Treatment of amounts deductible under section 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, or 179E
children.label: B Treatment of amounts deductible under section 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, or 179E

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of computing the earnings and profits of a corporation, except as provided in clause (ii), any amount deductible under section 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, or 179E shall be allowed as a deduction ratably over the period of 5 taxable years (beginning with the taxable year for which such amount is deductible under section 179, 179B, 179C, 179D, or 179E, as the case may be).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: In the case of a corporation that is a real estate investment trust, any amount deductible under section 179D shall be allowed in the year in which the property giving rise to such deduction is placed in service (or, in the case of energy efficient building retrofit property, the year in which the qualifying final certification is made).

children.heading: Certain foreign corporations
children.label: 4 Certain foreign corporations
children.text: The provisions of paragraph (1) shall not apply in computing the earnings and profits of a foreign corporation for any taxable year for which less than 20 percent of the gross income from all sources of such corporation is derived from sources within the United States.

children.heading: Basis adjustment not taken into account
children.label: 5 Basis adjustment not taken into account
children.text: In computing the earnings and profits of a corporation for any taxable year, the allowance for depreciation (and amortization, if any) shall be computed without regard to any basis adjustment under section 50(c).

children.heading: Does not increase earnings and profits if applied to reduce basis
children.label: 1 Does not increase earnings and profits if applied to reduce basis
children.text: The earnings and profits of a corporation shall not include income from the discharge of indebtedness to the extent of the amount applied to reduce basis under section 1017.

children.text: Sections 173 and 248 shall not apply.

children.label: A In general
children.text: Earnings and profits shall be increased or decreased by the amount of any increase or decrease in the LIFO recapture amount as of the close of each taxable year; except that any decrease below the LIFO recapture amount as of the close of the taxable year preceding the 1st taxable year to which this paragraph applies to the taxpayer shall be taken into account only to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: LIFO method
children.label: i LIFO method
children.text: The term “LIFO method” means the method authorized by section 472 (relating to last-in, first-out inventories).

children.heading: Inventory assets
children.label: ii Inventory assets
children.text: The term “inventory assets” means stock in trade of the corporation, or other property of a kind which would properly be included in the inventory of the corporation if on hand at the close of the taxable year.

children.heading: Installment sales
children.label: 5 Installment sales
children.text: In the case of any installment sale, earnings and profits shall be computed as if the corporation did not use the installment method.

children.heading: Completed contract method of accounting
children.label: 6 Completed contract method of accounting
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer who uses the completed contract method of accounting, earnings and profits shall be computed as if such taxpayer used the percentage of completion method of accounting.

children.heading: Redemptions
children.label: 7 Redemptions
children.text: If a corporation distributes amounts in a redemption to which section 302(a) or 303 applies, the part of such distribution which is properly chargeable to earnings and profits shall be an amount which is not in excess of the ratable share of the earnings and profits of such corporation accumulated after , attributable to the stock so redeemed.February 28, 1913

children.heading: Definition of original issue discount and issue price for purposes of subsection (a)(2)
children.label: o Definition of original issue discount and issue price for purposes of subsection (a)(2)
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(2), the terms “original issue discount” and “issue price” have the same respective meanings as when used in subpart A of part V of subchapter P of this chapter.
